title: Updating the Knowledge Base
concept: Updating or merging new reflected insights with existing semantic memory without creating conflicts.

exercises.prompt: What do we do during Memory Reflection?

options.text: We summarize episodes and extract lessons-learned.
We forget everything we learned before.
We only focus on new information without review.

exercises.explain: During Memory Reflection, we summarize our experiences and learn from them, enhancing our understanding.

cards.title: What is Knowledge Base Updating?
cards.body: Updating the knowledge base means adding new information or insights to what we already know without causing confusion.

cards.title: Updating Knowledge Base
cards.body: This process involves merging new insights into existing memory, ensuring we do not create contradictions in our understanding.

cards.title: Like Upgrading Software
cards.body: Think of updating your knowledge base like upgrading software. You add new features but keep existing ones intact and functioning smoothly.

cards.title: Practical Example
cards.body: Consider learning about a new species of animal. You would add this new information to what you already know about animals instead of replacing everything.

cards.title: Myth: New Knowledge Means Old Knowledge Is Wrong
cards.body: It's a myth that new insights invalidate old ones. Instead, they can enhance and deepen your understanding.

cards.title: How Knowledge Base Updates Work
cards.body: Visualize it as a tree: existing branches represent your current knowledge, while new branches for new insights keep growing on the same tree.

right.text: New branches grow alongside old ones.

exercises.prompt: What is a benefit of updating our knowledge base?

options.text: It allows us to integrate new information smoothly.

exercises.explain: Integrating new information smoothly helps us have a richer understanding without losing what we've already learned.

exercises.prompt: Updating the knowledge base is only about forgetting old information.
exercises.explain: False! Updating the knowledge base means adding to what you know, not just forgetting.

exercises.prompt: To update our knowledge base, we merge new insights with our existing __________.
exercises.sentence: To update our knowledge base, we merge new insights with our existing semantic memory.
exercises.explain: Semantic memory is the correct term because it refers specifically to our stored knowledge.

exercises.prompt: Which statement shows proper knowledge updating?

options.text: I learned that giraffes have long necks, which contradicts what I know about animal size.
I learned that jellyfish are not fish; they are more like invertebrates, enhancing my understanding of sea creatures.
I learned new facts and now forget everything I knew about ocean life.

exercises.explain: Correct! The second option properly updates knowledge by adding to existing information without contradiction.

exercises.prompt: What is essential when updating our knowledge base with new insights?

options.text: Always aim for contradictions to create challenges.
Integrate new insights without conflicting with old understanding.
Replace old knowledge with new information completely.

exercises.explain: Integrating new insights without conflicting ensures a cohesive and enriched knowledge base, allowing for deeper understanding.
